methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Tubulis is a Series C-stage biotech & health company that has raised €436M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 private companies at the same stage and sector (Ultima Genomics at $4.0B, Insitro at $2.4B, Colossal Biosciences at $1.5B), supplemented by reference to 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) trading at a median price-to-sales multiple of 6.1x.

Only one quantitative method proved applicable: peer-median triangulation. The median valuation across the three same-stage, same-sector private peers stands at $2.4B, with a measured range from $1.5B (low) to $4.0B (high). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be deployed because Tubulis does not publicly disclose revenue figures, a constraint typical of private-stage companies. The peer-median approach thus carries the full analytical weight.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.8B, centered on $2.4B. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue run-rate and growth trajectory relative to peers, capital efficiency metrics, and stage-specific risk factors not captured in static peer multiples. The single-method reliance underscores that triangulation depth is limited by data availability at the private stage.
